sourceMovie.title: The Dark Knight
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Dark Knight (2008) stuck because it commits to its action premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Christopher Nolan's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The action and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Dark Knight Rises
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Dark Knight clicked for you, The Dark Knight Rises (2012) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Dark Knight?
faq.answer: Try The Dark Knight Rises, Payback, Batman: Year One—each shares DNA with The Dark Knight while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Dark Knight?
faq.answer: The Dark Knight (2008) is best described as Action, Crime, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Dark Knight part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "The Dark Knight Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
